title: Background workers — auto-triggered maintenance daemon (lite)
source: ruvnet/ruflo — 12 auto-triggered background workers (audit, optimize, testgaps, persona-refresh, etc.) that fire on schedule or idle. Adapted to our scale: skip the daemon, just provide a `workers run` command driven by a JSON schedule that the user (or a cron / sleepy loop) invokes. Keeps Rick's project healthy without manual maintenance commands.
description: Rick has lots of one-shot maintenance commands (persona check, prometheus scan, sleepy stats) but nothing fires them automatically. Inspired by ruflo's worker registry, add `scripts/workers.py` with a small registry of maintenance tasks driven by `.cto/workers.json` (per-worker `interval_hours` + `last_run`). Calling `python scripts/workers.py run` executes any worker whose interval has elapsed. The user wires this into cron or a sleepy hook. Three default workers ship: stale_ticket_worker (flag tickets in_progress > 48h), persona_drift_worker (call persona refresh if stale), prometheus_idle_worker (run prometheus scan if last scan > 24h ago).
proposed_changes:
Create a NEW file `scripts/workers.py` with this structure:

1. Imports: argparse, json, subprocess, sys, datetime, pathlib.Path. Reuse `find_cto_root` by importing from orchestrate.py (try/except ImportError fallback to inline copy).

2. WORKER_REGISTRY dict mapping name -> (callable, default_interval_hours):
   - 'stale_ticket': (run_stale_ticket_worker, 24)
   - 'persona_drift': (run_persona_drift_worker, 12)
   - 'prometheus_idle': (run_prometheus_idle_worker, 24)

3. `def run_stale_ticket_worker(root: Path) -> dict`:
   Glob `.cto/tickets/*.json`. For each ticket with status=='in_progress', check `updated_at` (or `created_at` fallback). If older than 48h, append a marker line to `.cto/logs/stale_tickets.log` (TICKET-ID title age_hours). Return {'flagged': N, 'checked': total}.

4. `def run_persona_drift_worker(root: Path) -> dict`:
   subprocess.run(['python', str(root / 'scripts' / 'persona.py'), 'check'], capture_output=True, timeout=30, check=False). Return {'returncode': proc.returncode, 'stdout': proc.stdout.decode()[:200]}.

5. `def run_prometheus_idle_worker(root: Path) -> dict`:
   subprocess.run(['python', str(root / 'scripts' / 'prometheus.py'), 'scan'], capture_output=True, timeout=300, check=False). Return {'returncode': proc.returncode, 'output_tail': proc.stdout.decode()[-300:]}.

6. `def load_schedule(root: Path) -> dict`:
   Path .cto/workers.json. If missing, build from registry defaults: {name: {'interval_hours': default, 'last_run': null, 'enabled': true}}. Save and return.

7. `def save_schedule(root, sched): json.dump(...)`.

8. `def is_due(entry: dict) -> bool`:
   If not entry.get('enabled'): return False. If entry['last_run'] is None: return True. parsed = datetime.fromisoformat(entry['last_run']). return (now - parsed).total_seconds() / 3600 >= entry['interval_hours'].

9. `def cmd_run(args)`:
   root = find_cto_root(); sched = load_schedule(root). For each name, entry in sched.items(): if args.worker and args.worker != name: continue. if not is_due(entry): print(f'  {name}: skip (next in {hours_left}h)'); continue. fn, _ = WORKER_REGISTRY[name]. print(f'  {name}: running...'). result = fn(root). entry['last_run'] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(). entry['last_result'] = str(result)[:200]. save_schedule(root, sched). print(f'  {name}: done -> {result}'). Print summary line at end: '{ran}/{total} workers ran.'

10. `def cmd_list(args)`:
   sched = load_schedule(root). Print table: NAME | INTERVAL | LAST_RUN | DUE? | LAST_RESULT (one row per worker). Use plain text — no rich dependency required.

11. `def cmd_enable(args)` / `def cmd_disable(args)`:
   Toggle entry['enabled'] for args.worker. Save.

12. main(): argparse with subparsers run/list/enable/disable. `run` accepts --worker NAME (optional, single-worker run).

13. At top of file, add `if __name__ == '__main__': main()` guard.

No external deps — stdlib only. Catch and log all subprocess timeouts/errors so a failing worker never breaks the rest of the run. Do not touch prometheus.py or any other existing script.
